name: Lin Chong
description: Lin Chong, often referred to as 'The Panther Head,' is a prominent figure in Chinese folklore and a key character in the classic novel *Water Margin* (also known as *Outlaws of the Marsh*). While not strictly a deity in the traditional pantheon, Lin Chong occupies a liminal space, venerated as a righteous hero and protector, embodying themes of loyalty, betrayal, and resilience. His story resonates deeply with the common people, representing the struggle against corrupt officials and the yearning for justice. Lin Chong's transformation from a disciplined and respected Imperial Guard instructor to an outlaw on Mount Liang Shan speaks to the societal injustices prevalent during the Song Dynasty. He is often invoked for strength, perseverance, and the ability to overcome adversity, showcasing the enduring power of moral integrity in the face of overwhelming odds. His legacy is one of a tragic hero, forced into rebellion by circumstance, but ultimately remaining true to his core values.
subtitle: The Panther Head
titles: One of the 108 Stars of Destiny

keyMyths.title: Forced into Rebellion
keyMyths.description: Lin Chong's initial reluctance to join the outlaws of Liang Shan highlights his deep-seated loyalty to the Song Dynasty, despite the corruption within its ranks. He was framed by Gao Qiu, a high-ranking official, and his son, leading to his exile and eventual forced enrollment among the Liang Shan rebels. This event underscores the theme of moral compromise in the face of systemic injustice, forcing Lin Chong to choose between his duty and his own survival. This myth highlights the corruption of the Song Dynasty.

keyMyths.title: The Snowy Night Duel
keyMyths.description: While exiled, Lin Chong was targeted for assassination. This encounter, taking place on a snowy night, is a pivotal moment in his transformation from a loyal soldier to a hardened outlaw. He is forced to defend himself against assassins sent by Gao Qiu, showcasing his exceptional martial arts skills and his unwavering determination to survive. This event further solidifies his resolve to seek retribution against those who wronged him and his family.

keyMyths.title: Joining Liang Shan
keyMyths.description: After enduring numerous hardships and betrayals, Lin Chong ultimately joins the Liang Shan outlaws, becoming a key military leader. His expertise in combat and his strategic mind are invaluable assets to the rebel group. This signifies his complete break from the corrupt imperial court and his commitment to fighting for the oppressed and the downtrodden. He rises through the ranks to become a powerful and respected leader among the outlaws.

sources.author: Shi Nai'an

sources.author: Jackson, David D.
sources.description: While not directly about Lin Chong, this source provides historical context to the Song Dynasty setting of *Water Margin*, including the social and political climate that led to widespread corruption and rebellion.
